title: Boat Rental in Alicante: How to Choose the Right Boat for 2026
html:
When planning a boat rental in Alicante, the first step is matching the vessel to your group, budget, and preferred stretch of the Costa Blanca. From €150 for a half-day motorboat to €1,200 for a crewed yacht, options vary widely; start your search on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Alicante or list your own.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Alicante?

For most motorboats up to 6m length, a Spanish recreational boating licence is required, but some rental operators offer licence-free options for smaller, slower vessels. Spanish law mandates a licence for boats over 15 HP (about 11 kW) or longer than 6m. Many local outfits in Alicante provide short-term licences or include a skipper. Always confirm with the operator on BoatHire24 before booking. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

Where to set off in Alicante

Real Club Náutico de Alicante is the main hub, right next to the city centre and the Postiguet Beach. Marina Deportiva del Puerto de Alicante offers larger berths for catamarans and yachts. To the north, the exclusive marina of Puerto de Campello is a popular departure for day trips to the island of Isla de Tabarca. Further along the coast, Puerto de Santa Pola provides access to the protected salt flats and quieter coves. For a more laid-back start, the small fishing port of Villajoyosa (La Vila Joiosa) offers charm and calmer waters.

Which boat to rent in Alicante

Your choice depends on group size, desired activities, and budget; here are the four main categories.

  1. Motorboat (open or bowrider), best for day trips with a small group up to 6 guests; from €150 for half a day. Consider the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, 150 HP, a fast and stable option for exploring the coastline; see this speedboat on BoatHire24.
  2. Sailing catamaran (Lagoon or similar), ideal for families or two couples, up to 10 guests; from €600 for a full day with a skipper.
  3. Luxury yacht (Azimut, Fairline, Sunseeker), for corporate events or celebrations, capacity 10-12 guests; from €1,000 for a full day with crew.
  4. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), perfect for water sports and fast transfers, up to 8 guests; from €200 for four hours.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Alicante?

Half-day motorboat rentals start around €150, while full-day crewed catamarans average €700, with peak season prices up to 30% higher. Price bands vary by boat type and season.

  • Shared group tour (RIB or small motorboat, 2-3 hours): €50-80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests, no skipper): €150-250.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 10 guests, with skipper): €600-900.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 12 guests, with skipper and sometimes fuel): €1,000-1,500.
Alicante’s northern wind, the ‘Poniente’, is most common from June to September, making for calm seas and reliable sailing conditions; always check the local forecast before departing.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Alicante?

High season runs from June through September, when sea temperatures reach 24-26°C and the Poniente wind keeps the Mediterranean calm. July and August are the busiest months; book well in advance. May and October offer quieter conditions and lower prices, though the water is cooler. For 2026, expect a strong demand from international tourists, so early booking is wise.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €1,500 per trip
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Average trip length: 4 hours or full day
  • Typical guest capacity: 6, 12 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Alicante

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Alicante?

Some marina operators allow you to drive unlicensed if the engine is under 15 HP and the boat is small, but a licensed skipper is recommended for safety and insurance.

What is the best island to visit from Alicante?

Isla de Tabarca, a marine reserve about 8 nautical miles offshore, is a favourite for snorkelling and lunch in the only inhabited island village.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Alicante for 2026?

For peak summer weeks in July and August, book at least two to three months ahead; spring and autumn can be booked a few weeks prior.

Are fuel costs included in the rental price?

Fuel is usually extra on bareboat charters but often included on crewed yacht rentals; always confirm with the operator.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, Alicante’s marinas and offshore anchorages are best explored by water. Compare verified boat options on BoatHire24 and find the right vessel for your group. If you own a boat in Alicante, you can browse the listings and consider becoming a host to earn extra income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Alicante and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
